message:
Try a little experiment tonight: when your dog is relaxed and watching you, let out a big, dramatic yawn. 🐶😮 There's a real chance that within a few seconds… your dog yawns right back. And no, it's not a coincidence — it's science.

Researchers have found that dogs can "catch" human yawns, just like we catch them from each other. In one well-known study, 21 of 29 dogs yawned after watching a person yawn — but stayed perfectly still when the person just made random mouth movements. Some scientists believe this contagious yawning hints at a rudimentary form of empathy: a sign your dog is deeply tuned in to you and even mirrors what you're feeling. There's friendly debate among researchers about exactly how "emotional" it is, but the connection itself is very real.

Whatever the full explanation, it's a lovely reminder of just how closely our dogs read us. They watch our faces, sense our moods, and quite literally yawn along with us. 💛

So now you HAVE to try it — give a big yawn and watch your pup. Did they catch it? Come back and tell me if it worked! 🐾
